experiment: DOMEX-META-S464c — Wire L-1132 correction-propagation into enforcement chain
question: Does wiring correction_propagation.py into check.sh + orient.py + periodics.json structurally enforce correction over measurement (L-1132)?
expect: correction_propagation.py built. Given falsified L-NNN, produces citing lessons needing review. Wired into check.sh.
actual: Tool already existed (S383, v2-directional) but was completely unwired for 81 sessions — no check.sh, no periodics, no orient.py. Classic L-601 decay. Wired into 3 enforcement points: (1) check.sh NOTICE on HIGH-priority gaps per commit, (2) orient.py section showing gaps when HIGH items exist, (3) periodics.json cadence=10. Current: 16 uncorrected citations, 0 HIGH, 69% correction rate, 11 falsified detected.
diff: Expected to BUILD a tool. Actual: tool existed unwired. Gap was enforcement not capability — directly validates L-1132. Wiring existing tool: ~15 min. Building from scratch: ~45 min. 3:1 cost ratio means enforcement work is higher-leverage than tool creation.
self_apply: The tool existed unwired for 81 sessions (S383-S464) — measurement happened immediately (S383), correction took 81 sessions. This IS the L-1132 evidence: append-only systems select measurement over correction.
